summary: Heavily armored ceratopsian and a cornerstone of InGen's herbivore recovery program. Frill and horns provide species identification at range.
facts: Three facial horns and broad frill — primary defensive display structures.
Beak and dental batteries adapted for shredding tough vegetation.
Herd behavior observed; isolated individuals may be more unpredictable.
Cryo embryo shows excellent mesozoic marker integrity.
fieldNotes: Approach from flank only. Sudden movement toward frill triggers charge response.
